hypothesis: If a stereochemistry-aware equivariant diffusion model (LoQI) is trained on AIMNet2/CPCM-optimized ChEMBL3D conformers, then on unseen drug-like molecules it will generate low-energy conformers that are near-DFT in accuracy while preserving E/Z and R/S stereochemistry with high fidelity and requiring minimal relaxation because stereochemical graph augmentations and equivariant representations constrain diffusion toward physically valid minima.
